name: G.P.O.
description_short: The U.S. Government Publishing Office (GPO) is a federal agency that publishes, preserves, and provides free access to authentic government information and documents from all three branches of government.
description_long:
The U.S. Government Publishing Office (GPO) is a legislative branch agency with a 165-year history of government publishing and information dissemination. Established as a federal agency, GPO serves as the official publisher for Congress, the Executive Branch, and the Judicial Branch, producing essential government documents including the Congressional Record, Federal Register, Code of Federal Regulations, and the President's Budget. The agency operates as a non-commercial entity funded by congressional appropriation and user fees for printing and publishing services.

GPO's primary offerings include free public access to government information through GovInfo, a comprehensive digital repository that provides authentic, published documents from all three federal branches. The agency maintains the Federal Depository Library Program (FDLP), which disseminates government publications to libraries nationwide, and operates the U.S. Government Bookstore for public document purchases. GPO also provides printing and publishing services for federal agencies, manages the Catalog of U.S. Government Publications, operates Ben's Guide to the U.S. Government for civic education, and hosts Government Book Talk for public engagement.

What distinguishes GPO is its position as the sole official publisher of federal government documents and its commitment to making government information universally accessible at no cost through digital channels. The agency has digitized over 10,000 volumes of the Congressional Serial Set dating back to 1817, recorded over 15 billion retrievals of government information online (1.97 billion in FY2025 alone), and maintains the only authoritative source for published federal information. GPO has been recognized as America's Best Employer four consecutive years and Best Employer for New Graduates, reflecting operational excellence.

GPO is not a consumer finance company and is fundamentally misclassified in the payday-alternatives category. As a federal government agency providing free civic and legislative information, GPO serves a completely different function from consumer lending or financial assistance. The agency has no involvement in personal lending, credit services, or financial products of any kind. Consumers seeking government financial information or civics education resources may benefit from GPO's free offerings, but the company provides no financial products or services whatsoever.
services: GovInfo digital repository providing free government information access
Federal Depository Library Program (FDLP) with 1,200+ participating libraries
U.S. Government Bookstore for document sales and distribution
Printing and publishing services for federal agencies
Ben's Guide to the U.S. Government civic education platform
Catalog of U.S. Government Publications searchable database
Government Book Talk public engagement program
Congressional Record, Federal Register, and Code of Federal Regulations publishing
Digitization of Congressional Serial Set volumes
Government Integrated Print Services (GIPS) for agency documents
pros: Completely free access to authentic government documents and information through GovInfo platform
Over 15 billion retrievals recorded, demonstrating scale and reliability of government information access
10,000+ volumes of Congressional Serial Set digitized back to 1817 for historical research
Federal Depository Library Program provides widespread distribution through 1,200+ libraries nationwide
Recognized as America's Best Employer four years in a row, indicating organizational quality
Official publisher status guarantees authenticity and accuracy of all federal documents
Free civic education resources including Ben's Guide to the U.S. Government
cons: Not a financial services provider—completely misclassified as payday-alternatives company
No consumer finance products, lending services, or credit assistance available
Cannot assist with personal loans, debt relief, credit building, or emergency cash needs
best_for: Researchers and students seeking authentic federal government documents and historical records
Citizens wanting free access to Congressional records, legislation, and federal regulations
Libraries and educators using the Federal Depository Library Program resources
diagnosis: GPO is a federal government agency providing free access to authentic government documents and civic information—not a consumer finance company. This company is fundamentally misclassified and offers no financial products, lending services, or credit assistance of any kind.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
